programming: Requires dealer or locksmith with SLIS access and compatible programmer
notes: 2005-2009 Outback uses DAT17 blade with Texas 4D62 40-bit. Some 2009 may have 80-bit variant; verify before cloning.

programming: Requires dealer or locksmith with SLIS pin access; Lonsdor K518USA can pull pin codes
notes: Two FCC variants exist for this generation. CWTWBU766 at 434MHz (US) covers 2010-2014; CWTWB1U811 at 315MHz covers 2011-2014. Verify original FCC ID before replacing. Some 2010 models may still use the 4D62 40-bit transponder-only key.

programming: Requires professional programming; no onboard programming available
notes: 2015-2019 Outback transitions from DAT17 to B110 blade. FCC CWTWB1U811 spans a wide model range at 315MHz. Proximity/smart key option also available on higher trims.

programming: No onboard programming; requires dealer or locksmith with advanced programmer
notes: 2020+ Outback uses 8A/H chip DST-AES equivalent. Smart key (push-to-start) standard; bladed transponder on base trims. Emergency blade B110 included in smart key. HYQ14AHK confirmed at 434MHz.

programming: Requires dealer or locksmith with compatible programmer
notes: 2004-2008 Forester uses 4D62 40-bit. 2008-2011 transition: some units use 4D62 40-bit, some may use 80-bit G chip variant. Always verify chip before cloning.

programming: Requires professional programming with compatible tool
notes: 2012-2018 Forester uses B110 blade with G/4D60 80-bit chip at 315MHz. 2014-2018 specifically confirmed on multiple sources.

programming: No onboard programming; requires dealer or advanced locksmith programmer
notes: 2019+ Forester uses 8A H chip DST-AES. Smart key standard on most trims. B110 emergency blade included in smart key. HYQ14AHK confirmed at 434MHz.

programming: Requires professional programming; no onboard procedure
notes: XV Crosstrek (2013-2017) and Crosstrek (2018) use B110 blade with G/4D60 chip at 315MHz. Confirmed 2013-2017 range.

programming: No onboard programming; requires dealer or advanced locksmith programmer
notes: 2019+ Crosstrek uses 8A H chip. Smart key standard. Confirmed 2019-2023 with HYQ14AHK at 434MHz.

programming: Requires dealer or locksmith with compatible programmer
notes: Impreza 2004-2010 uses DAT17 with 4D62 40-bit. 2011 may use 4D62-6F (80-bit G chip) variant. Verify chip.

programming: Requires professional programming; no onboard procedure
notes: 2012-2016 Impreza uses B110 blade at 315MHz. Confirmed on multiple aftermarket sources.

programming: No onboard programming; requires dealer or advanced locksmith programmer
notes: 2017+ Impreza 8A H chip. Smart/proximity standard; bladed transponder on base trims. HYQ14AHK confirmed at 434MHz.

programming: Requires dealer or locksmith with compatible programmer
notes: Legacy 2005-2009 DAT17 blade with 4D62 40-bit. Consistent with Outback same generation.

programming: Requires Lonsdor K518USA for pin; professional programming required
notes: 2010-2014 Legacy matches Outback generation exactly. DAT17 blade with G chip 4D60 80-bit. CWTWBU766 confirmed at 434MHz for US market.

programming: Requires professional programming; no onboard procedure
notes: 2015-2019 Legacy moves to B110 blade with G chip at 315MHz. Smart key available on higher trims.

programming: No onboard programming; requires dealer or advanced programmer
notes: 2020+ Legacy uses 8A H chip. Push-to-start standard. B110 emergency blade in smart key. OEM PN 88835-AN00A confirmed by UHS-Hardware.

programming: No onboard programming; requires dealer or advanced programmer
notes: Ascent launched 2019. Smart key HYQ14AHK primary at 434MHz; CWTB1G077 remote head key variant at 433.92MHz (listed as 434MHz by industry convention) with H chip and B110 blade. HYQ14AKB also available on some years at 434MHz.

programming: Requires dealer or locksmith with compatible programmer
notes: WRX/STI 2008-2014 shares Impreza platform; DAT17 with 4D62 chip.

programming: Requires professional programming
notes: 2015-2021 WRX/STI uses B110 blade with G chip at 315MHz. Remote head key standard on WRX.

programming: No onboard programming; requires dealer or advanced programmer
notes: 2022+ WRX new generation uses H chip 8A with CWTB1G077 FCC on remote head key at 433.92MHz (listed as 434MHz by industry convention). B110 blade.

programming: Requires professional programming; no onboard procedure
notes: BRZ gen 1 (2013-2020) uses TR47 blade (also known as TOY43/X217; different from most Subaru B110/DAT17). G chip 4D60 80-bit at 315MHz. Shared platform with Toyota 86/Scion FR-S. All sources confirm HYQ12BBY coverage ends at 2020.

programming: No onboard programming; requires dealer or advanced programmer
notes: 2022+ BRZ new generation switches to B110 blade and 8A H chip at 434MHz, matching mainstream Subaru lineup. FCC ID HYQ14AHK confirmed, OEM PN 88835-FL032.

programming: Can self-program with 2 working keys (for remote); transponder requires locksmith or dealer
notes: 2004-2013 Mazda3 uses MAZ24 blade with Texas 4D63 80-bit (DST+). Aftermarket replacement keys supplied as 80-bit; original early 2004-2006 vehicles may have 40-bit OEM chip but 80-bit is backward compatible for programming. 80-bit cannot be cloned with standard equipment.

programming: No onboard programming; requires Mazda VCM II or equivalent advanced programmer
notes: 2014+ Mazda3 push-to-start. Hitag Pro ID49 chip (NOT ID47; ID47 is Hyundai/Kia). Emergency MAZ24 blade included in smart key. WAZSKE13D01 confirmed across multiple sources.

programming: Requires Mazda VCM II or advanced programmer; no DIY option
notes: 2019+ Mazda3 upgraded to HITAG AES (6A chip). FCC WAZSKE11D01 confirmed. WAZSKE11D01 = HITAG AES; WAZSKE13D01/D03 = HITAG Pro ID47/ID49.

programming: Can self-program remote with 2 keys; transponder requires locksmith or dealer
notes: Mazda6 2003-2013 without push-to-start uses MAZ24 blade with 4D63 80-bit. Identical to Mazda3 same era. 80-bit cannot be cloned with standard equipment.

programming: No onboard programming; requires Mazda VCM II or equivalent
notes: 2014+ Mazda6 push-to-start standard. Hitag Pro ID49 (NOT ID47). Shares FCC/part with Mazda3 same era.

programming: Requires Mazda VCM II or advanced programmer
notes: 2019+ Mazda6 uses WAZSKE13D03 with NXP HITAG Pro ID49 chip (NOT HITAG AES). HITAG AES is only on WAZSKE11D01 models. Confirmed by locksmithkeyless.com and multiple supplier listings.

programming: No onboard programming; requires Mazda VCM II or equivalent
notes: First-gen CX-5 (2013-2018) is push-to-start smart key only. Hitag Pro ID49 (NOT ID47). FCC WAZSKE13D01 confirmed across transpondery.com and multiple suppliers.

programming: Requires Mazda VCM II or advanced programmer; no DIY option
notes: Second-gen CX-5 (2019+) uses WAZSKE13D03 with NXP HITAG Pro ID49 chip (NOT HITAG AES/PCF7953M). HITAG AES is only on WAZSKE11D01 models. Confirmed by locksmithkeyless.com.

programming: Can self-program remote with 2 keys; transponder requires locksmith or dealer
notes: CX-9 2007-2015 uses MAZ24 blade with 4D63 80-bit. 80-bit cannot be cloned with standard equipment.

programming: No onboard programming; requires Mazda VCM II or equivalent
notes: Second-gen CX-9 (2016+) push-to-start only. Hitag Pro ID49 (NOT ID47). FCC confirmed WAZSKE13D01/WAZSKE13D02.

programming: Requires Mazda VCM II or advanced programmer
notes: 2020+ CX-9 uses WAZSKE13D03 with NXP HITAG Pro ID49 chip (NOT HITAG AES). Confirmed at 315MHz.

programming: Requires Mazda VCM II or advanced programmer; no DIY option
notes: CX-30 launched 2020 with HITAG AES (6A chip) from the start. FCC WAZSKE11D01 confirmed. Shares key with Mazda3 2019+ and CX-50.

programming: Requires Mazda VCM II or advanced programmer
notes: CX-50 launched in US market for 2023MY. HITAG AES 6A chip, FCC WAZSKE11D01. Shares key with CX-30 and Mazda3 2019+.

programming: Can self-program remote with 2 keys; transponder requires locksmith or dealer
notes: MX-5 NC (2006-2015) uses MAZ24 blade with 4D63 80-bit. 80-bit cannot be cloned with standard equipment.

programming: No onboard programming; requires Mazda VCM II or equivalent
notes: MX-5 ND (2016-2019) push-to-start smart key. Hitag Pro ID49 (NOT ID47). Confirmed shares FCC WAZSKE13D01 with Mazda3/6 same era.

programming: Requires Mazda VCM II or advanced programmer
notes: MX-5 ND facelift (2020+) uses WAZSKE13D03 with NXP HITAG Pro ID49 chip (NOT HITAG AES). Confirmed at 315MHz.

programming: No onboard programming; requires Mazda VCM II or equivalent
notes: CX-3 US market 2016-2020. Hitag Pro ID49 (NOT ID47). FCC WAZSKE13D01 confirmed across multiple sources.

programming: Requires Mazda VCM II or advanced programmer
notes: 2021 CX-3 confirmed with WAZSKE13D03 (FCC). Uses NXP HITAG Pro ID49 chip (NOT HITAG AES). Note: CX-3 was discontinued in US after 2021.
